emotional_core: The moment you stop protecting someone from your life and realize they belong everywhere you belong, dissolving the boundaries between all the parts of yourself.
visual_metaphor: Two people under a blanket in darkness with warm candlelight, fully present to each other while a family circle forms around them—safety as enclosure, aliveness as presence, integration as belonging.
key_quotes: she said you made my heart feel safe, but you make it feel alive
The twinkle in your eyes can rival that of a star when you talk about her
Then dissolving in my arms out of sheer love and embarrassment

story_frames.visual: Wide shot of a couple under a blanket in darkness, soft candlelight casting warm glow on their faces. They're outside under stars or inside a cozy room.
Close-up of one person's face, eyes closed, relaxed. Candlelight dancing on their skin. Pure comfort.
Same person's eyes now open, wide awake, fully present. Intensity in the gaze. Fully alive.
Both people's faces visible under the blanket. Eyes meeting. Neither asleep nor performing. Just present.
Shift to a family table or living room—the beloved sits with the viewer's mama, laughing, fully themselves. The viewer watches from the edge of frame.
The viewer moves into frame, sitting between their mama and their beloved. All three together. Circles collapse into one.
Soft fade to the original blanket and candlelight scene, but now both people are glowing—safe, alive, integrated.
